Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi Kang Apr 14th, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Updates on the spatial analysis •Attempts with existing tools on patch-level spatial analysis •Revisit the scale differences between TMA cores and WSI •Let’s try to plan a framework for ROIs selection •Other things •RPU science day presentation -> we will be noticed •Too high occupancy of Linux workstation, delayed progress -> Next Cyclops meeting conflict with CancerBio kickoff -> I need to announce that in the slack •SPACEstat test on EyeMT project with Aleksandra and Elias •SCSO abstract •NC review DDL on Wed •What is the Kauppi collab meeting on April 23rd? •iMTB report

To describe tissue landscape: 1. Cell-cell interaction scale 2. Global immune-TSI distance 3. Immune from stroma/tumor side 4. Network structure analysis 5. Automated / semi-automated ROIs selection!

MESA (multiomics and ecological spatial analysis)https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-025-02119-z

MESA (multiomics and ecological spatial analysis)https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-025-02119-z
GDI: global diversity index

MESA on TMA cores - Colorectal Cancer, CODEX
Shannon Diversity IndexMoran's I
https://mesa-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/notebooks/tutorials/MESA_tutorial_ecospatial_murine.html#Perform-Ecospatial-Analysis-on-one-sampleVisualise hot/cold spots: spatial clustering of high or low local Moran’s I values surrounded by similar valuesCalculate centroids of hot/cold islands and label them (DPI)Use scale = 32 The unit is in µm “Tissue samples are dissected into gridded patches across a range of scales, denoted as a series (1/2)^n (n = 0, 1, 2, …), to facilitate multiscale analysis. “

Bray-Curtis distance: assess the cellular composition between two islandsMESA on TMA cores - Colorectal Cancer, CODEX

Workstation crashed…out of RAM

MESA on WSI images - with 3 smallest images, HRD vs HRP

TMA vs WSI image
Here in µm
TMA vs WSI, different scales -Perhaps we should focus on different spatial patterns. -Spatial point pattern (e.g. Ripley’s K) not working, too time-consuming -MESA toolkit also time-consuming But with WSI we can: (1) Long distance spatial relationship (2) Immune networks, (3) Global immune-TSI difference, etc.. -> results more solid than TMA cores

Framework of SPACEstat? 
1.Point-level analysis (in this step we will be able to combine all images, and find the clinical relevant spatial features) •Overview of cell-cell interaction •Basic spatial metrics calculation •TSI interface identification •Immune infiltration index + TSI-distance statistical tests •Functional marker enrichment analysis •[TBF] Immune network component analysis •[TBF] global point pattern analysis - optimize the K/L functions 2.Patch-level analysis with the size of TMA cores (this step should be per-image level) •ROI selection: Rank the patches based on user’s preferences, using geographical toolkits •If no preferences: tissue heterogeneity or best representative? (Learn from Antti) •Immune infiltration •Tumor functional markers •Etc… •P .S. Allow jitter in ROI selection •Clinical relevant spatial features enrichment analysis among patches
Why SPACEstat special? A toolkit for WSI image, computational friendly; The ﬁrst automated ROIs selection solution for WSI images, etc..

Let’s book the next meeting: May 1st or 2nd. By that time I should: 1.Finish the deconvolution of bulk RNA-seq 2.Finish the development of patch ranking function in SPACEstat; Thanks for listening!
